title: dactylaena microphylla
intro: Dactylaena microphylla, also known as the 'cinnamon bush', is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dactylaena microphylla include relief from digestive issues, arthritis, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to lower blood sugar levels and aid in weight management. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts that can be used to support overall health and well-being.
